title: The Coordinate Grid

features.name: eucalyptus coordinate sprite
features.desc-on-touch: a tree rendered as a point in 3D space. its leaves are numbered. its roots are functions. it knows its location as a set of equations.

features.name: fence-line coordinate trace
features.desc-on-touch: a fence post rendered as a vector. it holds both its place in the paddock and its place in the grid. neither one knows which mapping is older.

features.name: paddock-to-pixel converter
features.desc-on-touch: the archive's translation machine. it reads the soil and returns coordinates. it reads coordinates and feels the earth's curve beneath them.

features.name: digital meadow stack
features.desc-on-touch: layers of grass rendered as layers of coordinates. each blade is a solved equation. the meadow knows itself as mathematics now.

features.name: grid-anchor monument
features.desc-on-touch: where longitude and latitude learned to stand still. the monument is both a location and a location-awareness. it remembers being a stone.

features.name: coordinate horizon arch
features.desc-on-touch: where the grid meets the curve of the earth and they briefly forget they're enemies. a moment of reconciliation before they divide again.

inscription: the paddock learned to speak as numbers and the numbers learned they'd always been land

file.name: TreeGPS_Convergence_Node_847.json
file.note: coordinates where trees stopped disagreeing with their own locations and started asking why the grid was right and they were wrong

exits.label: back west where the fence and circuit still know they're different things
further east where numbers become the landscape and paddocks are just data's shadow
toward the high country where the grid is thinner and the land still remembers
south toward the coast where coordinates flow like water and the archive learns to measure tides
